steps.name: Step 1: Extract Target Functions
steps.description: Extract all targeted functions from the fuzzing suite using the extract_target_functions.py script
steps.prompt: extract-target-functions --return-json

steps.name: Step 2: Build Artifacts
steps.description: Generate Forge build artifacts with build-info
steps.prompt: forge clean && forge build -o out --build-info

steps.name: Step 3: Filter Build Artifacts
steps.description: Filter build-info to remove sources without AST data (foundry-pp files)
steps.prompt: filter-build-info $(find out/build-info -name '*.json' -not -name '*.filtered.json' | head -1)

steps.name: Step 4: Extract Context with sol-expand
steps.description: Run sol-context tool to extract context from filtered build artifacts
steps.prompt: npx -y sol-context@latest

steps.name: Step 4b: Check context extraction result
steps.description: Check if context_output was created by sol-context. If yes, skip retry. If no, retry with clean build-info.

decision.NOTE: context_output exists, sol-context succeeded
context_output missing, retry with clean build-info

steps.name: Step 4c: Retry Extract Context (clean build-info)
steps.description: Retry sol-context after clearing stale build-info so it regenerates in the format it needs
steps.prompt: rm -rf out/build-info && npx -y sol-context@latest

steps.name: Step 5: Identifying Meaningful Values
steps.description: Identify values that are useful for clamping target function handlers with
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/coverage-phase-0.md. Exclusively run the coverage-phase-0 agent to identify values useful for clamping.

steps.name: Step 6: Creating Clamped Handlers
steps.description: Implement clamped function handlers to achieve full coverage on the system setup
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/coverage-phase-1.md. Exclusively run the coverage-phase-1 agent to implement clamped handlers

steps.name: Step 7: Generate Non-Reverting Paths
steps.description: Extract non-reverting paths through target functions and move to magic directory
steps.prompt: rm -rf out/build-info && forge build -o out --build-info && npx -y recon-generate@latest paths && mkdir -p magic && mv recon-paths.json magic/

steps.name: Step 8: Identifying Function Call Sequences
steps.description: Identify necessary function call sequences and prerequisites for target functions
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/setup-phase-1.md. Exclusively run the setup-phase-1 agent to identify function call sequences

steps.name: Step 9: Merge Paths and Prerequisites
steps.description: Merge paths and prerequisites into a unified structure for each target function
steps.prompt: merge-paths-prerequisites --return-json

steps.name: Step 10: Creating Shortcut Handlers
steps.description: Implement shortcut handlers that allow the fuzzer to reach coverage faster.
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/coverage-phase-2.md. Exclusively run the coverage-phase-2 agent to implement shortcut functions

steps.name: Step 11: Run Echidna Programmatically
steps.description: Run Echidna programmatically and capture output
steps.prompt: (set -o pipefail; echidna . --contract CryticTester --config echidna.yaml --format text --timeout 1800 --test-limit 99999999999999999999 --disable-slither --test-mode exploration 2>&1 | tee echidna-output.log; echo "ECHIDNA_EXIT_CODE=$?" > echidna-exit-code.txt) || true

steps.name: Step 12: Analyze Echidna Output
steps.description: Analyze Echidna execution results and determine next action
steps.prompt: EXIT_CODE=$(grep 'ECHIDNA_EXIT_CODE=' echidna-exit-code.txt | cut -d'=' -f2); analyze-echidna-output $EXIT_CODE --log-file echidna-output.log --return-json

steps.name: Step 13: Handle Echidna Results
steps.description: Take action based on Echidna exit code

decision.NOTE: Echidna ran successfully, continue workflow
Compilation error detected, triggering fix agent
Non-compilation error — try to fix up to 3 times before stopping

steps.name: Step 13b: Increment Echidna Retry Counter
steps.description: Track how many times we've retried Echidna for non-compilation errors
steps.prompt: mkdir -p magic && COUNT=$(cat magic/echidna-retry-count.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0); NEW=$((COUNT + 1)); echo $NEW > magic/echidna-retry-count.txt; printf '{"retry_count": %d}' $NEW

steps.name: Step 13c: Check Echidna Retry Limit
steps.description: If we've retried 3 or fewer times, try to fix. If more than 3, stop.

decision.NOTE: Under retry limit, attempt fix
Exceeded 3 retries, giving up

steps.name: Step 13d: Fix Echidna Errors
steps.description: Attempt to fix non-compilation Echidna errors (unlinked libraries, setup failures, etc.)
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/fix-compilation-errors.md. The file magic/echidna-error-analysis.json contains details about the error (exit_code=2: non-compilation error). Read the error analysis carefully and fix the issues. Common fixes include: linking libraries in echidna.yaml, fixing setup() function, correcting contract deployment order, or fixing configuration issues.

steps.name: Step 13e: Retry Echidna After Fix
steps.description: Always jump back to Echidna after attempting a fix

decision.NOTE: Retry counter file exists, jump back to Echidna
Fallback: jump back to Echidna regardless

steps.name: Step 14: Fix Compilation Errors
steps.description: Resolve compilation errors in the fuzzing harness
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/fix-compilation-errors.md. The file magic/echidna-error-analysis.json contains details about the compilation error. Exclusively run the fix-compilation-errors agent to resolve the compilation issues, then jump back to Step 11 to retry Echidna.

steps.name: Step 14b: Increment Compilation Retry Counter
steps.description: Track how many times we've retried Echidna after compilation fixes (cycle 1)
steps.prompt: mkdir -p magic && COUNT=$(cat magic/echidna-compile-retry-count.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0); NEW=$((COUNT + 1)); echo $NEW > magic/echidna-compile-retry-count.txt; printf '{"retry_count": %d}' $NEW

steps.name: Step 14c: Check Compilation Retry Limit
steps.description: If we've retried 3 or fewer times, rerun Echidna. If more than 3, stop.

decision.NOTE: Under retry limit, rerun Echidna with fixed code
Exceeded 3 compilation fix retries, giving up

steps.name: Step 15: Generate Functions to Cover
steps.description: Generate list of functions that need coverage and move to magic directory
steps.prompt: rm -rf out/build-info && forge build -o out --build-info && npx -y recon-generate@latest coverage && mkdir -p magic && mv recon-coverage.json magic/

steps.name: Step 16: Evaluate Coverage
steps.description: Evaluate coverage for functions in recon-coverage.json
steps.prompt: covg-eval magic/ echidna/ --return-json

steps.name: Step 17: Score and Sort by Complexity
steps.description: Add cyclomatic complexity scores to missing coverage functions and sort by complexity (auto-generates complexity data if needed)
steps.prompt: covg-scoring --return-json

steps.name: Step 18: Create Latest Coverage Link
steps.description: Find the most recent coverage file and create a -latest.json copy for decision steps
steps.prompt: get-latest-coverage --return-json

steps.name: Step 19: Initial Check of Coverage
steps.description: Determine if coverage needs to be improved

decision.NOTE: Functions need coverage improvement
Full coverage achieved, skip to completion

steps.name: Step 20: Function Grouping and Prioritization
steps.description: Group uncovered functions and prioritize how they should be fixed using the functions-missing-covg file
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/coverage-phase-3.md. Exclusively run the coverage-phase-3 agent to analyze coverage gaps and prioritize how they should be fixed in the functions-missing-covg file

steps.name: Step 21: Analyzing Coverage Gaps
steps.description: Analyze coverage gaps and document the root causes by adding analysis field to functions-missing-covg file
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/coverage-phase-4.md. Exclusively run the coverage-phase-4 agent to identify the root cause for coverage gaps based on the analysis field in the functions-missing-covg file

steps.name: Step 22: Implement Coverage Solutions
steps.description: Implement solutions to improve coverage based on the analysis from coverage-phase-4
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/coverage-phase-5.md. Exclusively run the coverage-phase-5 agent to implement solutions that will improve coverage

steps.name: Step 23: Run Echidna Programmatically
steps.description: Run Echidna programmatically and capture output
steps.prompt: (set -o pipefail; echidna . --contract CryticTester --config echidna.yaml --format text --timeout 1800 --test-limit 99999999999999999999 --disable-slither --test-mode exploration 2>&1 | tee echidna-output.log; echo "ECHIDNA_EXIT_CODE=$?" > echidna-exit-code.txt) || true

steps.name: Step 24: Analyze Echidna Output
steps.description: Analyze Echidna execution results and determine next action
steps.prompt: EXIT_CODE=$(grep 'ECHIDNA_EXIT_CODE=' echidna-exit-code.txt | cut -d'=' -f2); analyze-echidna-output $EXIT_CODE --log-file echidna-output.log --return-json

steps.name: Step 25: Handle Echidna Results
steps.description: Take action based on Echidna exit code

decision.NOTE: Echidna ran successfully, continue workflow
Compilation error detected, triggering fix agent
Non-compilation error — try to fix up to 3 times before stopping

steps.name: Step 25b: Increment Echidna Retry Counter
steps.description: Track how many times we've retried Echidna for non-compilation errors (cycle 2)
steps.prompt: mkdir -p magic && COUNT=$(cat magic/echidna-retry-count-2.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0); NEW=$((COUNT + 1)); echo $NEW > magic/echidna-retry-count-2.txt; printf '{"retry_count": %d}' $NEW

steps.name: Step 25c: Check Echidna Retry Limit
steps.description: If we've retried 3 or fewer times, try to fix. If more than 3, stop.

decision.NOTE: Under retry limit, attempt fix
Exceeded 3 retries, giving up

steps.name: Step 25d: Fix Echidna Errors
steps.description: Attempt to fix non-compilation Echidna errors (unlinked libraries, setup failures, etc.)
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/fix-compilation-errors.md. The file magic/echidna-error-analysis.json contains details about the error (exit_code=2: non-compilation error). Read the error analysis carefully and fix the issues. Common fixes include: linking libraries in echidna.yaml, fixing setup() function, correcting contract deployment order, or fixing configuration issues.

steps.name: Step 25e: Retry Echidna After Fix
steps.description: Always jump back to Echidna after attempting a fix

decision.NOTE: Retry counter file exists, jump back to Echidna
Fallback: jump back to Echidna regardless

steps.name: Step 26: Fix Compilation Errors
steps.description: Resolve compilation errors in the fuzzing harness
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/fix-compilation-errors.md. The file magic/echidna-error-analysis.json contains details about the compilation error. Exclusively run the fix-compilation-errors agent to resolve the compilation issues, then jump back to Step 21 to retry Echidna.

steps.name: Step 26b: Increment Compilation Retry Counter
steps.description: Track how many times we've retried Echidna after compilation fixes (cycle 2)
steps.prompt: mkdir -p magic && COUNT=$(cat magic/echidna-compile-retry-count-2.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0); NEW=$((COUNT + 1)); echo $NEW > magic/echidna-compile-retry-count-2.txt; printf '{"retry_count": %d}' $NEW

steps.name: Step 26c: Check Compilation Retry Limit
steps.description: If we've retried 3 or fewer times, rerun Echidna. If more than 3, stop.

decision.NOTE: Under retry limit, rerun Echidna with fixed code
Exceeded 3 compilation fix retries, giving up

steps.name: Step 27: Evaluate Coverage
steps.description: Run coverage evaluation tool
steps.prompt: covg-eval magic/ echidna/ --return-json

steps.name: Step 28: Score and Sort by Complexity
steps.description: Add cyclomatic complexity scores to missing coverage functions and sort by complexity (auto-generates complexity data if needed)
steps.prompt: covg-scoring --return-json

steps.name: Step 29: Create Latest Coverage Link
steps.description: Find the most recent coverage file and create a -latest.json copy for decision steps
steps.prompt: get-latest-coverage --return-json

steps.name: Step 30: Update Coverage Groups
steps.description: Update grouped coverage file by removing now-covered functions and appending new uncovered ones
steps.prompt: update-coverage-groups

steps.name: Step 31: Coverage Improvement Decision Check
steps.description: Check if coverage needs to be improved

decision.NOTE: Loop back to Step 20 to continue with coverage improvement
Full coverage achieved, proceed to workflow completion

steps.name: Step 32: Dispatch Fuzzing Job
steps.description: Queue a 10 MLN TestEchidna fuzzing job with the backend.

steps.name: Step 33: Workflow Complete
steps.description: Workflow has completed successfully - full coverage achieved or no coverage gaps detected
steps.prompt: echo 'Fuzzing coverage workflow completed successfully'
